pattern: The fewest groups equals the maximum number of intervals overlapping at any single point, found by sweeping start and end events in time order.
hints: Picture the intervals on a timeline; how many overlap at the busiest moment?
That busiest-point count is exactly the number of groups you cannot avoid.
Sort start events (+1) and end events (-1) by coordinate, processing an end at x before a start at x+1, and track the running maximum of simultaneous intervals.

edge_inputs.description: all disjoint fit in one group
shared endpoint chain at the same point

stress.description: many short intervals over a narrow range to build deep overlap
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    out = []
    for _ in range(n):
        a = rng.randrange(1, 2000)
        b = a + rng.randrange(0, 50)
        out.append([a, b])
    return [out]
stress.note: narrow coordinate band forces high simultaneous overlap
